content.problemStatement:
Suppose a contest has NNN problems, with difficulties A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​, and we have a parameter XXX. The contest is said to be balanced if and only if the following conditions hold :


Ai≥Ai−1A_i \ge A_{i - 1}Ai​≥Ai−1​ for all 2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N, i.e. the problems are sorted in difficulty order.
Ai−Ai−1≤XA_i - A_{i - 1} \le XAi​−Ai−1​≤X for all 2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N, i.e. the difficulties between 222 adjacent problems differ by at most XXX.
Right now, your contest has problems of difficulties B1,B2,...,BNB_1, B_2, ..., B_NB1​,B2​,...,BN​. You sorted them in difficulty order, so Bi≥Bi−1B_i \ge B_{i - 1}Bi​≥Bi−1​ holds.

You want to modify the problems to ensure that the contest becomes balanced. Suppose the final contest difficulties were C1,C2,...,CNC_1, C_2, ..., C_NC1​,C2​,...,CN​. Then, the cost of this contest is max⁡i=1N(∣Ci−Bi∣)\max_{i = 1}^{N}(|C_i - B_i|)maxi=1N​(∣Ci​−Bi​∣). Minimize the cost while achieving a balanced contest. It can be proven that a balanaced contest always exists. Please note that problems are even allowed to have negative difficulties.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and XXX - the size of the array and the parameter.
The second line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,...,BNB_1, B_2, ..., B_NB1​,B2​,...,BN​




Output Format
For each test case,




output on a new  line : KKK the minimum cost of a balanced contest




output on the next line : NNN integers, C1,C2,...,CNC_1, C_2, ..., C_NC1​,C2​,...,CN​ representing a balanced contest acheiveing the minimum cost KKK. The following conditions should hold for CCC :



−2⋅109≤Ci≤2⋅109- 2 \cdot 10^9 \le C_i \le 2 \cdot 10^9−2⋅109≤Ci​≤2⋅109
Ci≥Ci−1C_i \ge C_{i - 1}Ci​≥Ci−1​
Ci−Ci−1≤XC_i - C_{i - 1} \le XCi​−Ci−1​≤X



If several such contests exists, output any.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all tests does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : C=[6,8,10]C = [6, 8, 10]C=[6,8,10] are valid contest difficulties because C1≤C2≤C3C_1 \le C_2 \le C_3C1​≤C2​≤C3​ , C2−C1=2≤KC_2 - C_1 = 2 \le KC2​−C1​=2≤K and C3−C2=2≤KC_3 - C_2 = 2 \le KC3​−C2​=2≤K.
Further, the cost of this array is 111 as the maximum value of ∣Ci−Bi∣|C_i - B_i|∣Ci​−Bi​∣ is 111.
Test case 3 : The array represents an already valid contest.
